title: Pandas: Filtering DataFrames with Boolean Logic
content:
### Day 64: Pandas: Filtering DataFrames with Boolean Logic

**Boolean Filtering** — the most common Pandas operation:
```python
df[df['salary'] > 100000]           # single condition
df[(df['age'] > 25) & (df['dept'] == 'Eng')]  # multiple
```

**Operators:** `&` (and), `|` (or), `~` (not). Always use parentheses!
codeExample:
# Day 64: Pandas: Filtering DataFrames with Boolean Logic
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({
    'name': ['Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave','Eve'],
    'age': [28, 32, 26, 45, 29],
    'salary': [120000, 80000, 95000, 110000, 87000],
    'dept': ['Eng','Mkt','Eng','Mkt','Eng']
})

# Single condition
high_earners = df[df['salary'] > 100000]
print("High earners:\n", high_earners)

# Multiple conditions (use & and parentheses!)
young_eng = df[(df['age'] < 30) & (df['dept'] == 'Eng')]
print("\nYoung engineers:\n", young_eng)

# isin()
selected = df[df['dept'].isin(['Eng', 'Data'])]
print("\nEng/Data:\n", selected)

# String contains
df2 = df[df['name'].str.startswith('A')]
print("\nNames starting with A:\n", df2)
keyTakeaways: Review the provided code sandbox to understand execution flow.
